title: Aldama nesomii
intro: Aldama nesomii, also known as Nesom's Aldama, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Aldama nesomii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Aldama nesomii is classified within the family Rosaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family.
